source_author_title: Reis, Seo, and Hassan, Molten Salt Flow Visualization to Characterize Boundary Layer Behavior and Heat Transfer in a Natural Circulation Loop
equation_or_form: P0 replication_reis_jadyn property mode; carry rho, mu, cp, k as named inputs
valid_range_or_source_limit: TAMU/Reis replication convention; not a new property correlation
tamu_overlap: Current Salt2/3/4 rows have direct replication-mode rows
allowed_use: Use for replication and for separating property-choice effects before any fitting
excluded_use: Do not mix with updated viscosity/cp/k sources inside one fitted residual

source_author_title: Sohal et al., Engineering Database of Liquid Salt Thermophysical and Thermochemical Properties; Janz et al., Molten Salts data compilations
valid_range_or_source_limit: General molten-salt engineering database; source package does not make it the TAMU replication mode
tamu_overlap: Current rows report Re ratio 1.0 and Pr/Gz ratios about 1.08-1.09 versus replication
allowed_use: Use as an updated-property reference/sensitivity lane after declaring mode
excluded_use: Do not use to fit friction or thermal residuals without a separate rerun and declared lane

source_author_title: Jin et al., Accurate Viscosity Measurement of Nitrates/Nitrites Salts for Concentrated Solar Power; Parida and Basu, On the Specific Heat Capacity of HITEC-Salt Nanocomposites for Concentrated Solar Power Applications; Santini et al., Measurement of Thermal Conductivity of Molten Salts in the Range 100-500 C
valid_range_or_source_limit: Updated candidate properties; first-order reduction only, no full 1D rerun in package
tamu_overlap: Current rows move mean Re to about 1.25-1.30x replication and Pr/Gz to about 0.46-0.61x
allowed_use: Use to bound property sensitivity and decide whether updated-property rerun is required
excluded_use: Do not fit K, f_D, Nu, UA, or HX residuals while treating property movement as fixed truth

source_author_title: Shah and London, Laminar Flow Forced Convection Heat Transfer and Flow Friction in Straight and Curved Ducts; Shah, A Correlation for Laminar Hydrodynamic Entry Length Solutions for Circular and Noncircular Ducts
equation_or_form: Fully developed laminar pipe reference f_D = 64/Re; entrance/reset length gate
valid_range_or_source_limit: Fully developed or entry-length reference; requires section development evidence
tamu_overlap: TAMU rows are low-Re but many sections have resets, fittings, recirculation, or section-effective flags
allowed_use: Report as reference side columns and reset/development diagnostics
excluded_use: Do not use as one global loop friction multiplier or as default for disturbed spans

source_author_title: Muzychka and Yovanovich, Pressure Drop in Laminar Developing Flow in Noncircular Ducts: A Scaling and Modeling Approach
equation_or_form: Developing pressure loss as fRe(L+) from reset distance and characteristic length
valid_range_or_source_limit: Laminar developing-flow model; implementation constants and characteristic length require audit
tamu_overlap: Reset map has x_from_reset and L/D rows, but thermal reset status remains unknown for wall/material changes
excluded_use: Do not collapse component, cluster, branch-apparent, and redevelopment losses into one fitted f_D

source_author_title: Lin et al., Measurement Methods and Techniques for Pressure Losses in Pipe Fittings; Lin et al., State-of-the-Art Review on Measurement of Pressure Losses of Fluid Flow Through Pipe Fittings; Salehi et al., Experimental Determination and Computational Fluid Dynamics Predictions of Pressure Loss in Close-Coupled Elbows (RP-1682); Patino-Jaramillo et al., Laminar Flow and Pressure Loss in Planar Tee Joints: Pressure Loss Coefficients
equation_or_form: Named losses: straight_section, component_K, cluster_K, branch_apparent with explicit pressure_basis and velocity_basis
valid_range_or_source_limit: Method source; current package includes upper-bound or section-effective CFD reductions, not transferable constants
tamu_overlap: Current rows classify 33 named losses; many universal names rejected and several K rows remain coarse/no-GCI or recirculation-adjacent
allowed_use: Use named loss ledger and H1-type bounded screens with fit/validation separation
excluded_use: Do not export universal K without geometry, pressure basis, velocity basis, and validity admission

source_author_title: Muzychka and Yovanovich, Laminar Forced Convection Heat Transfer in the Combined Entry Region of Non-Circular Ducts
equation_or_form: Forced combined-entry Nu as a function of laminar entry nondimensional groups
valid_range_or_source_limit: Source overlap package marks Re/Pr class inside, but characteristic-length conversion and constants are unaudited
tamu_overlap: TAMU low-Re rows overlap model class; several spans remain recirculation-invalid or coarse/no-GCI
allowed_use: Use as a reference or candidate after conversion audit and thermal admission
excluded_use: Do not tune internal Nu to absorb jacket, passive loss, radiation metadata, heater efficiency, wall storage, or residual heat

source_author_title: Chen et al., Characteristics of the laminar convective heat transfer of molten salt in concentric tube
equation_or_form: Low-Re molten-salt mixed-convection Nu using Gz, GrPrD/L, viscosity ratio, and concentric-tube assumptions
valid_range_or_source_limit: Package range: Re 300-2300; Pr 11-27; Gr 8.56e4-3.95e6; Gz 150-310; 35<L/D<95; concentric-tube molten salt
tamu_overlap: Current TAMU rows are outside or unknown: Re about 50-69 and L/D often about 4.7-16.8 in audited rows
allowed_use: Use only as future sensitivity if a branch is inside every audited group and BC/geometry match is documented
excluded_use: Do not promote for current Salt2/3/4 low-Re TAMU closure rows

source_author_title: Everts and Meyer, Laminar Hydrodynamic and Thermal Entrance Lengths for Simultaneously Hydrodynamically and Thermally Developing Forced and Mixed Convective Flows in Horizontal Tubes

source_author_title: Tian et al., Numerical analysis of turbulent mixed convection heat transfer of molten salt in horizontal tubes with uniformly cooled heat flux
valid_range_or_source_limit: Turbulent source; exact audited numeric range not promoted for low-Re TAMU laminar branches

equation_or_form: Heat-loss network: R_int + R_wall + R_contact + R_ins plus external convection and radiation path; jacket path separate
valid_range_or_source_limit: Architecture source, not a single fitted TAMU UA
tamu_overlap: TAMU heat ledger separates jacket, passive convection, rcExternalTemperature radiation metadata, heater input, wall/storage, and residual
allowed_use: Use to structure boundary/HX/wall-layer model forms and separate ledgers
excluded_use: Do not fit one global UA or internal Nu to hide external losses or wall/storage residuals

source_author_title: Reis, Seo, and Hassan, Consequences of Molten Salt Solidification in a Natural Circulation Flow Visualization Loop Due to Heater Failure
equation_or_form: Separate active cooling, heater behavior, passive loss, storage, and failure/transient terms
valid_range_or_source_limit: TAMU-loop architecture evidence; current steady package does not admit transient fitting
tamu_overlap: Current heat ledger has cooler/jacket removal, heater realized/imposed ratio, passive paths, and residual rows
allowed_use: Use to separate imposed-cooler replay from predictive HX/cooler and heater terms
excluded_use: Do not use CFD cooler duty as a predictive runtime input except in explicitly labeled imposed-cooler replay

source_author_title: VDI Heat Atlas; TAMU CFD rcExternalTemperature evidence from AGENT-277/AGENT-287
equation_or_form: Radiation is embedded/inseparable in current CFD rcExternalTemperature wallHeatFlux; independent 1D radiation would be epsilon*sigma*A*(T_s^4 - T_sur^4) only if modeled separately
valid_range_or_source_limit: CFD package has emissivity/Tsur metadata but no separate exported qr heat term
tamu_overlap: TAMU current wallHeatFlux includes rcExternalTemperature effect; heat ledger labels Q_radiation_bound_W as inseparable
allowed_use: Use as metadata-bounded diagnostic and prevent double counting
excluded_use: Do not add a separate radiation heat-loss term on top of realized CFD wallHeatFlux replay; do not set radiation to zero without a bound

source_author_title: Shen et al., Convective Heat Transfer of Molten Salt in Receiver Tube with Axially and Circumferentially Non-Uniform Heat Flux; Chen et al., Flow and Mixed Convection Heat Transfer of Hitec Salt in Multi-Sided Heating Pipes

valid_range_or_source_limit: Source geometries/heating patterns are not automatically TAMU-active

allowed_use: Use to justify wall-temperature, heat-flux pattern, and near-wall shell diagnostics
excluded_use: Do not convert nonuniform-heating source behavior into a global internal Nu correction
